strategic_profile.executive_summary: Purdue University-Main Campus (West Lafayette, IN) is a large public R1 land-grant flagship that has built its national brand on engineering, computer science, and STEM at scale, leading the Big Ten in engineering reputation while holding tuition flat for over a decade. It pairs elite STEM programs with a deliberately growth-managed, increasingly selective admissions posture and a research enterprise that crossed $1 billion in total expenditures for the first time in FY2025. Its identity is value-driven, employer-facing, and research-heavy rather than prestige-by-exclusivity.
strategic_profile.market_position: Positioned as the premier public engineering/STEM value flagship in the Midwest: it leads the 18-member Big Ten in engineering reputation, has held undergraduate tuition frozen for 13+ years (positioning on affordability), and competes nationally with peers like Illinois, Michigan, Georgia Tech, and Texas A&M for STEM talent. Strength is concentrated in engineering, computer science (via the 'Purdue Computes' initiative), agriculture, and applied sciences rather than across all liberal-arts domains. as_of: Fall 2025 / FY2025.
strategic_profile.admissions_philosophy: Capacity-managed selectivity rather than artificial scarcity. After 14 consecutive years of record demand, Purdue admitted 43.4% of ~86,953 applicants for Fall 2025 — described in reporting as its most selective class on record — and signaled further, significant reductions in the admit rate to avoid over-enrollment. Selectivity is highly differentiated by college (Engineering ~34.7% vs Education ~70.5%) and by residency (in-state notably higher than out-of-state/international), reflecting a land-grant mission to serve Indiana while managing growth. as_of: Fall 2025 cycle (Class of 2029); Fall 2026/Class of 2030 figures not yet final.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Purdue's College of Engineering is the leading engineering program in the Big Ten and ranks among the very top nationally (No. 5 overall in the U.S. News 2025 best graduate engineering edition per Purdue and search reporting), with 12 graduate programs ranked top-10 in the 2026 U.S. News lists.
analyst_takeaways.implication: For STEM-focused applicants, Purdue functions as a 'target/reach' for its name-brand engineering value; the school's prestige is program-specific (engineering/CS) rather than uniform, so fit assessments should weight intended major heavily.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Purdue's strategic positioning leans hard on cost: a multi-year undergraduate tuition freeze plus its 'most innovative' / value framing in national rankings differentiates it from peers that compete primarily on exclusivity.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Strong net-price and ROI story makes Purdue attractive to merit- and value-sensitive families; it is a defensible 'high-floor' financial choice relative to private STEM peers.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Purdue surpassed $1 billion in total research expenditures for the first time in FY2025 (up 12% YoY, ~40% over three years), bucking a flat/declining trend at many peer research universities, and signed an up-to-$250M Eli Lilly AI-drug-discovery collaboration.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Rising research intensity expands undergraduate research access, industry pipelines, and graduate-school positioning — a tailwind that strengthens Purdue's competitive standing against better-ranked peers.

analyst_takeaways.insight: Acceptance rate is tightening structurally because of capacity management, not just demand — Purdue has explicitly signaled 'further and significant reduction' in admit rate, with sharp by-college and by-residency differentiation.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Out-of-state and international STEM applicants should treat Purdue as more competitive than the headline ~43% rate suggests; in-state applicants retain a meaningful access advantage.

analyst_takeaways.insight: The West Lafayette campus is a walkable, self-contained college town in north-central Indiana near I-65, with a free Purdue Campus Transit system (launched Aug 2025, three fixed routes plus on-demand) and a small university-owned airport, but limited big-city amenities nearby.
analyst_takeaways.implication: Students get a focused, low-friction on-campus experience and easy intra-campus mobility, but those wanting a major-metro environment will rely on ~1-2.5 hour trips to Indianapolis or Chicago.

campus_dynamics.social_environment: Vibrant, organization-driven social scene anchored by 1,000+ student orgs and one of the largest Greek communities in the Big Ten (90+ Greek organizations; roughly ~18% of men and ~22% of women participating per secondary reporting). Greek life and student orgs are the primary social channels; the surrounding West Lafayette town offers limited nightlife, so campus events, athletics (Big Ten), and clubs carry the social weight. as_of: cited from 2025-era secondary sources (Niche, Purdue Exponent).
campus_dynamics.transportation_impact: Intra-campus mobility is strong and low-cost: Purdue Campus Transit (operated by SP+, launched Aug 14, 2025) is free for students/faculty/staff, with three fixed routes (Ross-Ade, Purdue Mall, Discovery Park loops) Mon-Fri 7a-7p plus on-demand service to outlying lots/airport, and subsidized CityBus passes ($49/semester) to downtown Lafayette. The trade-off is regional access: the nearest major airport is Indianapolis (~1 hour); Chicago airports are ~2-3 hours, so off-campus/long-distance travel requires planning. as_of: Fall 2025 transit schedule (primary Purdue Operations source).
campus_dynamics.research_impact: Research is a defining and rising part of the student experience. With FY2025 total research expenditures crossing $1 billion (up 12% YoY) and flagship initiatives like 'Purdue Computes' and Discovery Park District, undergraduates have expanding access to research labs, industry-sponsored projects (e.g., the up-to-$250M Lilly-Purdue 360 AI initiative), and graduate-level mentorship — strengthening pathways into top graduate programs and industry. as_of: FY2025 (ending June 30, 2025), primary Purdue newsroom source.

current_status.test_policy_details: Test-required: SAT or ACT required to be considered for admission

academic_structure.structure_type: College/School-based admission with two common-entry pathways: (1) First-Year Engineering (FYE) — West Lafayette engineering students are admitted to the College of Engineering, not to a specific major, and Transition-to-Major (T2M) after completing FYE requirements; (2) Exploratory Studies for undecided students. All other colleges admit students by major (direct admit). as_of_cycle: 2026-2027 catalog (catoid=19) for the program/college list; CODO/admission-restriction details pinned per-program below.

colleges.name: College of Agriculture
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Land-grant agriculture college spanning engineering, science, economics, and natural-resource tracks. Generally accessible for direct admit; strong path for students wanting STEM rigor within an applied/agricultural context. Note Agricultural & Biological Engineering BSAGE/BSBE degrees here are ABET engineering degrees housed in Agriculture, not the College of Engineering.
colleges.housing_profile: West Lafayette campus housing (residence halls and learning communities); not verified per-college from catalog
colleges.student_archetype: Students drawn to applied life sciences, food/agriculture systems, environmental and natural-resource work, and ag-business.

colleges.name: Mitch Daniels School of Business
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Per the 2025-2026 University Catalog Business CODO page, 'Major is open with no anticipated space restrictions.' Internal transfer (CODO) into business requires a minimum cumulative GPA of 2.5 plus a 3.0 GPA across specified business prerequisite courses. Combines well with the Integrated Business & Engineering (IBE) program for cross-disciplinary students.
colleges.housing_profile: West Lafayette campus housing; not verified per-college from catalog
colleges.student_archetype: Pre-professional business students (accounting, finance, marketing, supply chain, analytics) and quantitatively-minded economics students.

colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Teacher-licensure and educational-studies pathways; most programs lead to Indiana teaching licensure (non-licensure General Education tracks also exist). Direct entry; CODO available.

majors.name: Special Education Mild and Intense Intervention P to 12

colleges.name: College of Engineering
colleges.admissions_model: First-Year Engineering (FYE) common entry at West Lafayette — students are admitted to the College of Engineering, not a specific major, then Transition-to-Major (T2M) after completing FYE requirements. Indianapolis students are directly admitted to their major.
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: West Lafayette applicants apply to 'First-Year Engineering,' not to a named major. Per the College of Engineering enrollment policy, students are not admitted into a specific engineering degree program until they complete all FYE requirements (nine courses with GPA >= 2.00, EAI >= 2.00, and a grade of at least C- in each course). Transition to Major (T2M) runs three times/year (December, May, August); some schools (e.g., ME, AAE) have GPA/EAI thresholds for guaranteed first-choice admission. Plan for a strong first-year GPA, especially for high-demand schools.
colleges.housing_profile: West Lafayette campus housing including engineering learning communities; not verified per-college from catalog
colleges.student_archetype: Students committed to engineering who are comfortable with a competitive first-year common curriculum before specializing.

colleges.name: College of Health and Human Sciences
colleges.admissions_model: Direct admit by major for most programs; Nursing is a space-restricted secondary-admission program
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Broad college spanning nursing, health sciences, kinesiology, nutrition, psychology, public health, HDFS, hospitality, and speech/hearing. Most majors are direct admit, but Nursing (BSN) is space-restricted: per the catalog Nursing CODO page, 'Students are admitted on a SPACE AVAILABLE BASIS ONLY after a holistic review. Space is limited.' Pre-health students should target the health-sciences concentrations and watch CODO competitiveness for Nursing.
colleges.housing_profile: West Lafayette campus housing; not verified per-college from catalog
colleges.student_archetype: Pre-health and health-professions students, nursing applicants, and behavioral/social-science students focused on human well-being.

majors.name: Nursing
majors.admissions_pathway: Space-restricted; CODO admits on a SPACE AVAILABLE BASIS ONLY after holistic review (catalog 2024-2025 Nursing CODO)
prerequisite_courses: Six credits of biology and/or chemistry courses with labs, 3.0 or better

colleges.name: College of Liberal Arts
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Large, accessible college spanning humanities, social sciences, communication, and the Rueff School of Design, Art, and Performance (which includes BFA studio/professional programs that may have portfolio review). Generally direct admit; good home for double majors and flexible CODO. Note: BA in Artificial Intelligence and BA in Cognitive Science are housed in Philosophy here (distinct from the BS AI in the College of Science).
colleges.housing_profile: West Lafayette campus housing; not verified per-college from catalog
colleges.student_archetype: Humanities, social science, communication, language, and arts/design students, plus interdisciplinary and pre-law students.

colleges.name: College of Science
colleges.admissions_model: Direct admit by major; Computer Science, Data Science (CS), and Artificial Intelligence (BS) are space-restricted
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Houses math, the physical and life sciences, statistics, and the Department of Computer Science. CS, Data Science, and AI are explicitly high-demand and space-restricted: the CS department states all CODO approvals are made 'on a SPACE AVAILABLE BASIS' and that CS 18000 is restricted to CS/DS/AI majors only. Direct admission as a first-year is the most reliable route into CS — internal transfer is competitive. Other science majors (biology, chemistry, physics, math, statistics) are generally direct admit.
colleges.housing_profile: West Lafayette campus housing; not verified per-college from catalog
colleges.student_archetype: Quantitative and STEM students — computer scientists, mathematicians, statisticians, data scientists, and natural-science majors (many pre-health).

majors.name: Computer Science
majors.admissions_pathway: Direct admit strongly preferred; CODO on a SPACE AVAILABLE BASIS only (CS dept), highest consideration ~3.00 cumulative GPA
prerequisite_courses: CS 18000 (Problem Solving and Object-Oriented Programming) — required for CODO; A- recommended for highest consideration
Approved Calculus course — B or better recommended

Some programs are Indianapolis-only or statewide-location.

colleges.admissions_model: Direct admit by major for undergraduate Veterinary Nursing; the professional DVM is a separate graduate program
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Undergraduate degrees are in Veterinary Nursing (AAS and BSVN, including a distance-learning track). The professional DVM program is separate. Good fit for students pursuing veterinary nursing/technology careers.

colleges.name: John Martinson Honors College
colleges.admissions_model: Selective application/invitation; students earn a degree in a home college and complete Honors requirements
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: An overlay honors college — students major within another college and add Honors curriculum, research, and housing. Admission is selective (by application/invitation). No standalone undergraduate degree majors are listed in the catalog under this college.
colleges.housing_profile: Honors College residential community (Honors College and Residences); not verified in detail from catalog
colleges.student_archetype: High-achieving students across all majors seeking research, small seminars, and an honors living-learning community.

colleges.name: Libraries and School of Information Studies
colleges.admissions_model: No standalone undergraduate majors listed in the catalog (offers minors/certificates and graduate programs)
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Houses the School of Information Studies; per the catalog, no undergraduate degree majors are listed under this unit (it offers minors/certificates and graduate work). Relevant to students adding information-studies credentials.
colleges.housing_profile: Not applicable / not verified from catalog
colleges.student_archetype: Students adding information-studies minors/certificates alongside a major in another college.

colleges.name: Exploratory Studies (Department of Exploratory Studies)
colleges.admissions_model: Common entry for undecided students (Explorers, pre-program); students later CODO into a degree-granting major
colleges.strategic_fit_advice: Purdue's exploratory/undecided pathway. Students enter as 'Explorers' (a pre-program, not a degree) and transition into a degree-granting major. Good for students who want a structured year to choose, but note that high-demand targets (CS, Nursing) remain space-restricted on CODO.
colleges.housing_profile: West Lafayette campus housing; not verified from catalog
colleges.student_archetype: Undecided students wanting a structured exploration year before declaring a major.

majors.name: Explorers (pre-program)
majors.admissions_pathway: Common-entry exploratory pathway; not a degree-granting major

Sources: https://catalog.purdue.edu/content.php?catoid=19&navoid=25481 (Undergraduate Minors) and https://catalog.purdue.edu/content.php?catoid=19&navoid=25483 (Undergraduate Certificates)."}

application_deadlines.notes: Official first-year deadlines page: 'Aug. 1: Application opens: Apply using the Common App.' For Fall 2026 entry this is Aug. 1, 2025. The page does not print an explicit year.

Official first-year deadlines page: 'Nov. 1: Early Action application deadline.' Non-binding per CDS 2025-2026 C22 ('Do you have a nonbinding early action plan...?' = Yes). EA is also the deadline for full scholarship consideration and the John Martinson Honors College, and the priority deadline for competitive majors (Engineering, Computer Science, Flight, Nursing, Veterinary Nursing). For Fall 2026 entry this resolves to Nov. 1, 2025 (page shows no explicit year). NOTE: this falls in 2025, before the requested 2026-2027 window — reported with its true year, not adjusted.

Official first-year deadlines page: 'Jan. 15: Regular decision application deadline.' For Fall 2026 entry this resolves to Jan. 15, 2026, which falls inside the 2026-2027 window. Page shows no explicit year.

Official first-year deadlines page: 'Jan. 15: Early Action decision release: If you meet the Early Action deadline of Nov. 1, you'll receive an admission decision on Jan. 15.' Decision date, not an application deadline. Resolves to Jan. 15, 2026 for Fall 2026 entry.

Official first-year deadlines page: 'March 31: Regular decision release: If you meet the Regular decision deadline of Jan. 15, you'll receive an admission decision by March 31.' Decision date, not an application deadline. Resolves to Mar. 31, 2026 for Fall 2026 entry.

supplemental_requirements.details: Required. 250-650 words; choose from the 7 Common App prompts. Source: admissions.purdue.edu/apply/essay.php.

Required. Two Purdue-specific prompts, each 250 words max: (1) 'How will opportunities at Purdue support your interests, both in and out of the classroom?' and (2) 'Briefly discuss your reasons for choosing your major and your interest in studying at this campus location.' Source: admissions.purdue.edu/apply/essay.php.
Application includes self-reported high school grades and self-reported ACT or SAT scores. Source: admissions.purdue.edu/apply/essay.php.

Optional. Two prompts, each 500 words or fewer, on the honors experience and the interdisciplinary nature of the chosen field. Honors College consideration requires meeting the Nov. 1 Early Action deadline. Source: admissions.purdue.edu/apply/essay.php.

supplemental_requirements.target_program: Competitive majors: Engineering (West Lafayette first-year engineering); Engineering in Indianapolis (biomedical, computer, electrical, industrial, mechanical, motorsports engineering); Computer Science; Flight (professional flight technology); Nursing; Veterinary Nursing
supplemental_requirements.details: Nov. 1 is a priority deadline for these competitive majors; per the deadlines page, applications after Nov. 1 are accepted 'only if space allows.' Source: admissions.purdue.edu/deadlines/first-year-college-student/.
Not required. 'No letter of recommendation required - First Year' (Common App / Purdue first-year requirements).

holistic_factors.essay_importance: Very Important (CDS 2025-2026 C7 rates 'Application Essay' as Very Important)
holistic_factors.demonstrated_interest: Considered (CDS 2025-2026 C7 rates "Level of applicant's interest" as Considered)
holistic_factors.interview_policy: Not Considered in admission decisions (CDS 2025-2026 C7 rates 'Interview' as Not Considered)
holistic_factors.legacy_consideration: Not Considered (CDS 2025-2026 C7 rates 'Alumni/ae relation' as Not Considered)
holistic_factors.first_gen_boost: Considered (CDS 2025-2026 C7 rates 'First generation' as Considered)

major_selection_tactics: Apply to the major as the unit of admission, not just to Purdue — Purdue admits by major and reads your file against that program's pool and space.
Use the primary + alternate major/location slots deliberately — list a true second-choice major (and/or the Indianapolis location) as your alternate, not a throwaway.
For any engineering interest, apply to First-Year Engineering (FYE) — do NOT expect to pick a specific engineering discipline at the application stage.
If undecided (or as a strategic less-saturated entry point), apply to Exploratory Studies rather than forcing a competitive major you are unsure about.
Indicate Honors College interest on the application before the Nov 1 Early Action deadline if you want it — it is a separate, holistic opt-in tied to that deadline.
application_strategy.alternate_major_strategy: {"summary":"Purdue's alternate-major slot is a genuine safety net, but the real strategic weight is that changing into a space-restricted major AFTER you enroll (the CODO process) is hard and not guaranteed — so your primary/alternate choices at application time are high-stakes, not easily corrected later.","tactics":[{"point":"Treat your primary major choice as semi-binding: do not plan to 'get in anywhere and switch into CS (or another space-restricted major) later.'","rationale":"Computer Science (and other restricted majors) require a competitive change-of-major (CODO) that is space-available and non-appealable, with concrete academic bars. Banking on a post-enrollment switch is risky.","source_quote":"The majors in the Department of Computer Science are space restricted programs. ... CODO decisions are final and may not be appealed.","source_url":"https://cs.purdue.edu/undergraduate/codo.html","found":true},{"point":"Understand the concrete CODO bar before relying on it as a fallback: even meeting the minimums does not guarantee the switch.","rationale":"For CS, the highest-consideration benchmarks are an A- in CS 18000, a B or better in an approved Calculus course, and a minimum 3.00 cumulative GPA — but all approvals are space-available and reviewed against all applicants. This makes 'enroll in alternate, CODO into target' a real but uncertain path.","source_quote":"Students wishing to receive the highest consideration should earn an A- in CS 18000, a B or better in an approved Calculus course that we use to meet degree requirements, and have a minimum 3.00 cumulative GPA. ... all CODO approvals will be made on a SPACE AVAILABLE BASIS and will be determined through an academic review of all applicants","source_url":"https://cs.purdue.edu/undergraduate/codo-requirements.html","found":true},{"point":"Pick an alternate major you would actually be happy graduating in — ideally one in the same school as your target so a later internal change is more plausible.","rationale":"Purdue explicitly says to choose an alternate only if truly interested and that no major/location change is guaranteed; combined with the hard CODO reality, the alternate should be a destination you accept on its own merits, not merely a launchpad.","source_quote":"you should select an alternate choice of major only if you are truly interested in that program. Changing majors or locations is never guaranteed.","source_url":"https://admissions.purdue.edu/become-student/guide/","found":true},{"point":"Exploratory Studies is a valid 'soft' alternate for the undecided, but it is not a back door into space-restricted majors.","rationale":"It buys up to four terms to declare while keeping a four-year track, yet declaring into a restricted major still runs through the same space-restricted/CODO gate — so it relaxes timing, not selectivity.","source_quote":"up to four terms to declare your major ... Most students who begin their Purdue career in exploratory studies maintain a four-year graduation track.","source_url":"https://admissions.purdue.edu/majors/a-to-z/exploratory-studies-for-undecided-students.php","found":true}]}

financials.tuition_model: Public land-grant in-state/out-of-state tuition model. Purdue West Lafayette is a public university charging differentiated tuition for Indiana residents vs. nonresidents, and has held tuition frozen since 2012-13 (publicized as a multi-year tuition freeze). Specific tuition/COA dollar figures are intentionally not restated here — they come from the cost anchor (admissions.purdue.edu/cost-financial-aid/ and the Bursar fee-rate pages).
financials.aid_philosophy: Need-aware/merit-forward at a frozen-tuition public land-grant. The Division of Financial Aid states it is "committed to making Purdue's world-class education affordable, accessible and valuable to Boilermakers and their families," and the admissions cost page frames affordability around a long-running tuition freeze ("14 years of frozen tuition"). Merit scholarships are awarded by holistic review with automatic consideration for students who complete the admission application by the Nov. 1 Early Action deadline — no separate scholarship application for university-wide merit awards ("Students who complete the admission application ... by the Nov. 1 Early Action deadline are automatically considered for university-wide merit scholarships"). Merit is selective: "Each year about 15% of applicants receive a merit award." Need-based aid uses Federal Methodology (FAFSA required; CSS Profile not required). International students are not eligible for institutional financial aid. (Sources: purdue.edu/dfa/; admissions.purdue.edu/cost-financial-aid/; admissions.purdue.edu/cost-financial-aid/scholarships/; CDS 2025-2026 Section H, items H8/H14.)

scholarships.name: Trustees Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: Top university-wide merit award, determined by holistic review; renewable with a 3.0+ GPA; notification included with the admission decision.
scholarships.application_method: Automatic — no separate application; all admitted applicants who complete the admission application by the deadline are considered.
scholarships.deadline: Nov. 1 (firm) Early Action / admission deadline for Fall 2026 entry

scholarships.name: Presidential Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: University-wide merit award by holistic review; renewable with a 3.0+ GPA; notification included with the admission decision.
scholarships.application_method: Automatic — considered via the admission application; no separate scholarship application.
scholarships.deadline: Nov. 1 (firm) Early Action / admission deadline for Fall 2026 entry

scholarships.name: Lilly Scholars at Purdue Program
scholarships.benefits: Full-tuition award targeting underrepresented populations; renewable with a 3.0+ GPA; notification included with the admission decision.
scholarships.application_method: Requires an opt-in process during the admission application.
scholarships.deadline: Nov. 1 (firm) Early Action / admission deadline for Fall 2026 entry

scholarships.name: Indy Scholars Program
scholarships.amount: $10,000/year minimum (combinable with Presidential or Trustees scholarships)
scholarships.benefits: Renewable scholarship for students enrolling at Purdue in Indianapolis (requires a 3.00 GPA); additional benefits include guaranteed access to the Purdue Certificate in Entrepreneurship and Innovation plus career consulting (resume reviews, internship prep, interview coaching, networking).
scholarships.application_method: Automatic — list Indianapolis as first or alternate campus choice on the Common App and apply by the Early Action deadline; must accept the admission offer.
scholarships.deadline: Nov. 1 (firm) Early Action deadline for Fall 2026 entry

scholarships.name: National Recognition Programs Scholarship
scholarships.benefits: For students with a College Board National Recognition Programs distinction; domestic students only.
scholarships.application_method: Automatic — considered via the admission application.

credit_policies.philosophy: Purdue accepts transfer credit for college-level coursework completed at regionally accredited institutions with a qualifying grade, applying credit (not grades) toward a Purdue degree, while requiring substantial work to be completed in residence. Quoted criteria from the official transfer credit page: 'It was earned at a regionally accredited institution.' / 'It is college-level coursework (not remedial or developmental).' / 'You received a grade of at least a C-.' / 'Only credit transfers, your grades will not.' / Residency: 'at least 32 credit hours of upper-division courses must be earned at Purdue for a degree from the university.'

ap_policy.general_rule: No single universal minimum score is stated on the page; the policy uses subject-specific credit tables in which Purdue accepts AP scores of 3, 4, and 5, with higher scores generally awarding more course credit. Disclaimer quoted from page: 'AP transfer credit criteria are subject to change without notice.'
exceptions: {"subject":"Economics (combined Macroeconomics + Microeconomics)","found":true,"quote":"If you score a 3 or better on both the Macroeconomics and Microeconomics exams you will receive credit for ECON 21000. If you score a three on one and a four or five on the other, you will receive credit for ECON 21000 and credit for the appropriate ECON course as indicated above.","source":"https://admissions.purdue.edu/become-student/transfer/credit/ap/"}

{"subject":"Undistributed credit ('1XXXX' designation)","found":true,"quote":"Courses listed as '1XXXX' transfer as undistributed credit, meaning Purdue does not have a direct course equivalent or the minimum grade requirement for course equivalency has not been met.","source":"https://admissions.purdue.edu/become-student/transfer/credit/ap/"}
ap_policy.usage: Scores must be sent from College Board to Purdue to receive credit; undistributed credit is applied with advisor guidance. Quoted: 'To receive credit from AP exam results, you must request your official scores be sent electronically directly from College Board to Purdue University (code 1631).' Undistributed credit 'appears on your Purdue student record as credit hours within the respective subject area' and students should consult a Purdue academic advisor to determine how it may best be used to meet curriculum requirements.

ib_policy.general_rule: Quoted: 'Effective fall 2025, IB exams are scored on a 1–7 scale.' and 'Students must earn a score of 4 or higher to receive Purdue credit.' Eligibility quoted: 'Credit may be awarded for both Standard Level (SL) and Higher Level (HL) IB exams.' Submission quoted: 'To receive credit, you must request that your official IB scores be sent electronically directly to Purdue University West Lafayette from the IB organization.' Disclaimer quoted: 'IB transfer credit policies are subject to change without notice.'

tools: {"name":"Transfer Credit Course Equivalency Guide","found":true,"quote":"Our transfer credit course equivalency guide allows you to look up courses individually to determine how they may transfer to Purdue.","url":"https://sswis.mypurdue.purdue.edu/PurdueStudentSS/transferCreditCourseGuide/","source":"https://admissions.purdue.edu/become-student/transfer/credit/"}
transfer_articulation.restrictions: Transfer credit is accepted when: 'It was earned at a regionally accredited institution.' / 'It is college-level coursework (not remedial or developmental).' / 'You received a grade of at least a C-.' Grades do not transfer: 'Only credit transfers, your grades will not.' Residency requirement quoted: 'at least 32 credit hours of upper-division courses must be earned at Purdue for a degree from the university.' No maximum transfer-hour cap is stated on the page.

what_it_takes: {"point":"Engineering and CS are markedly more competitive than the university as a whole — community members repeatedly frame them as a 'high reach' even for strong applicants, with weed-out rigor once enrolled.","sources":["College Confidential","Reddit r/Purdue (via aggregated synthesis)","College Confidential chances threads (WebSearch summary)"],"verbatim_support":"College Confidential user 'vwlizard': 'Not to burst your bubble, but because you are applying for CS, all of those schools are a very high reach.' and referenced a peer with 'mid 1500's SAT, 3.8 GPA, and a couple of AP's that was turned down at Purdue, Cornell and CMU'; a separate CC thread summary noted Purdue engineering 'has an intensive weeding out process.'"}

{"point":"Aim high on test scores for the competitive STEM majors — community advice pushes well above the published middle 50%, with 1500+ SAT cited as a competitiveness threshold for CS.","sources":["College Confidential"],"verbatim_support":"CC user 'Mapk': 'I highly recommend you retake the SAT and aim for above 1500 your stats are not competitive for getting into CS at your desired schools.'","caveat":"This crossed the 'multiple sources' bar only partially — it is one strongly-stated CC opinion echoed in tone by the broader 'high reach' consensus, so weight it as a single-thread datapoint."}

{"point":"Application strength is holistic and curriculum rigor matters — take AP/IB and the strongest course load available; grades in courses related to your intended major and an upward trend are emphasized.","sources":["Quad Education (consultancy)","Niche/WebSearch admissions summary"],"verbatim_support":"Quad: admissions evaluate 'Grades related to your specific major and your potential to succeed,' 'Trends in the achievement of grades,' and 'Strength of your overall high school curriculum'; recommends taking 'AP or IB classes to showcase your academic breadth.'"}

{"point":"The supplemental essays carry real weight — community/consultancy consensus is that Purdue treats them as 'very important,' on par with GPA and course rigor, so they are a genuine lever, not a formality.","sources":["College Transitions (consultancy)","Quad Education (consultancy)"],"verbatim_support":"College Transitions: the supplemental essays are rated 'very important' and 'carry equal weight to GPA and secondary school rigor—more significant than test scores or recommendations.'"}
